questions.question: The three angles of a triangle measure 50°, 60°, and x°. What is the value of x?
questions.explanation: The sum of angles in a triangle is 180°. So 50 + 60 + x = 180, which gives x = 70.

questions.question: In a right triangle, one of the non-right angles measures 35°. What is the measure of the other non-right angle?
questions.explanation: A right triangle has one 90° angle. The other two angles must sum to 90°. So the missing angle is 90 − 35 = 55°.

questions.question: An isosceles triangle has a vertex angle of 40°. What is the measure of each base angle?
questions.explanation: In an isosceles triangle, the two base angles are equal. They sum with the vertex angle to 180°: 40 + 2b = 180, so b = 70°.

questions.question: Two angles of a triangle measure 48° and 67°. Is this triangle acute, right, or obtuse?
questions.explanation: The third angle is 180 − 48 − 67 = 65°. All three angles (48°, 65°, 67°) are less than 90°, so the triangle is acute.

questions.question: In triangle ABC, angle A = 25° and angle B = 90°. What is angle C?
questions.explanation: The sum of all angles is 180°. So 25 + 90 + C = 180, which gives C = 65°.

questions.question: An equilateral triangle has all sides equal. What is the measure of each angle?
questions.explanation: All three angles in an equilateral triangle are equal and sum to 180°. Each angle measures 180 ÷ 3 = 60°.
